tasks.title: Bootstrap voiceprint repo scaffold
tasks.description: Create a new private GitHub repo named `voiceprint`, clone it locally at ~/.voiceprint/, and lay down the repo structure: an empty `corpus/` directory (to hold per-source raw pulls, e.g. `corpus/github.md`, `corpus/linear.md`), a placeholder `voice-profile.md`, and a `README.md` documenting how to add a new corpus source and how to re-run the distillation pass. Commit and push the initial scaffold to main.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub repo `voiceprint` exists under Eric's account and is private
Repo is cloned locally at ~/.voiceprint/
~/.voiceprint/corpus/ directory exists (empty or with a .gitkeep)
~/.voiceprint/voice-profile.md exists as a placeholder
~/.voiceprint/README.md documents: (1) how to add a new corpus source, (2) how to re-run the distillation pass
Initial commit is pushed to main
tasks.summary: SUPERSEDED. Created private repo Eric-Lingren/voiceprint, cloned to ~/.voiceprint/, scaffolded corpus/, voice-profile.md placeholder, README.md, committed and pushed to main. Abandoned mid-run: Claude Code's auto-mode classifier flagged the plan (bulk verbatim company PR/review comments, including code blocks, leaving the org boundary into a personal repo) as a data-exfiltration pattern. Pivoted to a paraphrased-only, no-raw-storage approach with output moved into the public dotfiles repo instead. Local ~/.voiceprint deleted; remote repo needs manual deletion (see FU-001).

tasks.title: Pull GitHub comment corpus into corpus/github.md
tasks.description: SUPERSEDED — see summary. Original scope: using the `gh` CLI (authenticated as Eric-Lingren), pull the full available history of GitHub PR conversation comments, PR review (inline) comments, and PR review summary bodies authored by Eric-Lingren across repos in the standardmetrics org, excluding personal/non-work repos. Apply zero content filtering: keep code blocks, quoted reply text, and emoji-only or one-word acknowledgements verbatim. Do NOT pull commit messages or PR descriptions — those are explicitly excluded as voice-contaminated. Write the raw corpus to ~/.voiceprint/corpus/github.md, grouped by repo/PR, each entry preserving its source URL. Commit and push to main.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/github.md contains only comments authored by Eric-Lingren
corpus/github.md contains no commit messages and no PR description bodies
Entries include PR conversation comments, inline review comments, and review summary bodies
Each entry links back to its source GitHub URL
Comment text is verbatim (code blocks, quoted replies, emoji/one-word acks preserved, no filtering)
Commit is pushed to main
tasks.summary: SUPERSEDED (partial). Correct org identified as Quaestor-Technologies (not 'standardmetrics', which isn't a real GitHub org login). Pulled comments across all 40 org repos (Quaestor-Web bucketed by year to work around the search API's 1000-result cap; ~1036 PR threads total) into a transient scratchpad file, never committed to any repo. Killed partway through (432 lines / ~53 PRs collected) when the auto-mode classifier flagged the pattern. That partial pull was used directly as distillation input for T-0086, then deleted. No raw corpus file persists anywhere.

tasks.title: Pull Linear comment corpus into corpus/linear.md
tasks.description: SUPERSEDED — see summary. Original scope: pull the full available history of Linear issue comments authored by Eric across all teams and issues he has access to, with no time ceiling and zero content filtering (verbatim, including code blocks, quoted replies, and emoji/one-word acknowledgements). Write the raw corpus to ~/.voiceprint/corpus/linear.md, grouped by issue, each entry preserving its source Linear issue/comment link. Commit and push to main.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/linear.md contains only comments authored by Eric
Comments span all teams/issues Eric has access to, no time ceiling applied
Each entry links back to its source Linear issue/comment
Comment text is verbatim, no filtering applied
Commit is pushed to main
tasks.summary: SUPERSEDED (partial, paraphrased-only). Linear MCP access confirmed working this session (no key/CLI setup needed, see FU-002 resolution). Sampled ~40 Eric-authored comments across 4 of his 6 teams, ~28 issues, roughly Aug 2025-Jul 2026, excluding descriptions and bot/sync stub comments. Never persisted verbatim: read transiently via MCP tool calls inside a subagent's context, no raw corpus file written anywhere. Findings paraphrased directly into docs/voice-profile.md (4 new paste-ready rules, all 7 Layer 2 dimensions extended with Linear-derived signal, invented illustrative examples only, no real quotes/links/coworker or team names).

tasks.title: Distill first-pass voice-profile.md from the corpus
tasks.description: SUPERSEDED — see summary. Original scope: analyze corpus/github.md and corpus/linear.md end-to-end and write the two-layer voice-profile.md into ~/.voiceprint/, backed by verbatim quoted examples from both sources. Committed and pushed to main.
acceptance_criteria: voice-profile.md has two clearly separated layers: paste-ready rule block, then evidenced profile
Layer 1 contains between 15 and 25 imperative rules
Layer 2 covers all seven dimensions: register/formality, sentence shape, directness, warmth markers, hedging vs. assertion, structural tics, lexical fingerprint
Each dimension in layer 2 is backed by at least one quoted example sourced from corpus/github.md or corpus/linear.md
Commit is pushed to main
tasks.summary: Wrote docs/voice-profile.md in the public ~/.dotfiles repo (not ~/.voiceprint, which was abandoned). Now covers both GitHub and Linear signal (Linear folded in via T-0085 in a later session), per the proprietary-content pivot. All examples paraphrased/genericized: no verbatim code, no repo/PR/issue links, no coworker or team names. Layer 1 has 27 rules; Layer 2 covers all 7 dimensions with GitHub- and Linear-sourced observations noted separately per dimension. Not yet committed to git.
files_touched: claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md (moved from docs/voice-profile.md in a later session, wired into pr-revise Step 8b)

follow_ups.title: Delete the abandoned voiceprint GitHub repo
steps: Run: gh auth refresh -h github.com -s delete_repo (approve the browser device-flow prompt)

follow_ups.title: RESOLVED — set up Linear API access, then fold Linear signal into voice-profile.md
steps: DONE: Linear MCP was already connected/authenticated this session, no manual key needed.
DONE: pulled Eric's authored Linear comments transiently (subagent context only, never written to a file), paraphrased directly into docs/voice-profile.md.
